tldr: Bernard, both of your live ads are running the exact same headline, body, and CTA — functionally one creative duplicated twice, and the older one is already two days past your typical refresh window. Under Andromeda's clustering, two ads with identical copy register as a single concept, which is well below the five-concept floor Meta's retrieval engine needs to keep serving you efficiently.

benchmark.context: You're running 1 distinct concept across 2 ads — below the 5-concept floor Meta's Andromeda retrieval system uses for clustering since October 2025. Other LA fashion brands at your size refresh hero creative roughly every 21 days; the top performers are on a 14-day cycle, and at your scrape both ads are inside the danger window with identical copy.

ads.headline: Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here
ads.body: Shop the latest must-have looks from LA ✨ Get 15% OFF your first order — limited time.
drivers: 26 days running — already past your 28-day refresh window when you account for the duplicate sibling ad eating the same retrieval cluster.
Headline 'Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here' is generic — no product, no specificity, no hook. It's a category headline any apparel brand could run.
Body copy is identical to your second live ad. Andromeda treats two ads with verbatim copy as one concept, so spend is competing with itself instead of expanding reach.

ads.headline: Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here
ads.body: Shop the latest must-have looks from LA ✨ Get 15% OFF your first order — limited time.
drivers: Verbatim duplicate of your other live video — same headline, same body, same CTA, same offer. The retrieval engine collapses these into a single creative.
'Limited time' framing on a 17-day-old ad reads as evergreen scarcity — the platform has seen the same 'limited time' message running for over two weeks.
Both ads are video, both lead with the 15% first-order discount. There's no static, no carousel, no founder/styling angle — one format, one offer, one hook across the entire active set.

hero_concept.concept_name: ROSA babydoll — 'the cami that doesn't try'
hero_concept.hook: The cami you'll reach for before the going-out top.
hero_concept.headline: Lace trim. Zero effort.
hero_concept.primary_text:
ROSA is the V-neck babydoll cami we keep restocking — sheer enough to layer, soft enough to sleep in, finished with scalloped lace at the neckline and hem.

It's the piece that quietly becomes the most-worn thing in your drawer. Pair it with denim for a coffee run, or under an open blazer when you want the night to do the talking.

New customers get 15% off — code at checkout.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Flat-lay editorial: ROSA cami laid on warm-toned linen with a coffee cup, gold hoops, and folded denim styled around it — soft late-morning daylight, no model, product is the hero.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Both of your live ads are videos selling 'must-have looks from LA' with a generic discount headline — no product is named, no specific piece is shown long enough to anchor the creative. A static, product-led concept featuring an actual SKU breaks the duplicate-copy cluster and gives Andromeda a second distinct concept to test against your video.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a warm flat-lay scene on natural cream linen with a folded pair of light-wash denim, a small ceramic coffee cup, and a pair of gold hoop earrings arranged loosely beside the garment, with soft late-morning daylight raking across the fabric to catch the lace texture. Editorial fashion-commerce aesthetic, warm cream and bone color palette, soft shadows, slightly desaturated. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact pale ivory/off-white sheer mesh fabric and delicate cream lace trim of the ROSA cami exactly as shown — strictly NO white-out shift, NO cream-to-pink shift, NO yellow or beige cast on the mesh, NO color shift on the lace. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: V-neckline with scalloped lace trim along the V, delicate spaghetti straps, gathered empire-waist body that flows loose and sheer below the bust, scalloped lace trim along the bottom hem. Do NOT change strap thickness, do NOT alter V-neckline depth, do NOT remove or relocate the lace trim at the neckline or hem, do NOT change to a fitted cami silhouette, do NOT add buttons, ties, or hardware not present in the reference. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.
hero_concept.reference_title: ROSA V Neck Lace Trim Sheer Babydoll Cami Top

next_step.urgency: Both ads hit the danger window inside 8 days — and they're duplicates of each other.
next_step.headline: 30 minutes to map your next 5 concepts
next_step.body: Bernard, you're one concept deep against an algorithm that needs five. Let's walk through the angles we'd ship next — product-led statics, founder POV, styling carousels — and which ones to test first against your existing video.

method_note: Walked through every live ad on the Tic Toc Meta page, mapped fatigue against your refresh cadence and Andromeda's concept-clustering rules; no private account data used.
